NSAA-2018-M1-001: A group of drivers, consisting of 200 women and 300 men, was asked if they passed their driving test at the first attempt. Altogether 167 of the group said they passed at the first attempt. Of the women, 143 said they did not pass at the first attempt. How many of the men said they passed at the first attempt?
NSAA-2018-M1-002: A cuboid has sides of length x, 2x and (x + 2), measured in cm. The volume, in cm3, of the cuboid is numerically equal to twice the total surface area, in cm2, of the cuboid. What is the value of x?
NSAA-2018-M1-003: The line joining the points with coordinates (p, p - 1) and (1 - p, 2p) is parallel to the line with equation 2x + 3y + 1 = 0. What is the value of p?
NSAA-2018-M1-004: A rectangle PQRS is drawn inside a circle, with its vertices on the circumference of the circle. [DIAGRAM: rectangle PQRS inscribed in a circle]. The ratio of the length of PQ to the length of QR is 2:1. The area of the rectangle PQRS is 96 cm2. What is the radius, in cm, of the circle?
NSAA-2018-M1-005: The expected number of bottles of water sold in a day at a sports ground is directly proportional to the square of the average outside temperature, in degrees Celsius, for that day. On a day when the average outside temperature is 16°C, 64 bottles of water, the expected number, are sold. On a warmer day, when the average outside temperature is T°C, 256 bottles of water are sold, which is 31 bottles more than the expected number for that day. What is the value of T?
NSAA-2018-M1-006: At a cinema, drinks are sold in regular and large sizes. The cups for these are mathematically similar. The ratio of the heights of the cups and the ratio of the depths of the drinks are both 4:5. The volume of drink in a regular size cup is 320 cm3. What is the volume, in cm3, of drink in a large size cup?
NSAA-2018-M1-007: The mean of n numbers is p. The mean of two of these numbers is q. The mean of the remaining numbers is 10. Which of the following is a correct expression for n in terms of p and q?
NSAA-2018-M1-008: The straight lines 5x + 2y = 20, y = 3x - 23, and x = 0 enclose a region with area K square units. What is the value of K?
NSAA-2018-M1-009: A scale model of a cylindrical pillar is to be made. The full-sized pillar has a volume of 12π m3. The model will use a length scale of 1:40. The model is to be a solid cylinder made of a plastic which has a density of 4/3 g cm-3. What is the mass of the model in grams?
NSAA-2018-M1-010: PQRST is a regular pentagon [DIAGRAM: pentagon PQRST with equilateral triangle RSU constructed on side RS, external to the pentagon]. RSU is an equilateral triangle. What is the size of angle STU?
NSAA-2018-M1-011: The original price of an item is p. The price is increased by 125%. The increased price is then decreased by 40% to q. The relationship between p and q can be expressed as mp = q. What is the value of m?
NSAA-2018-M1-012: 80% of a number is equal to two-thirds of a second number. The whole number ratio of the first number to the second number in its lowest terms is x:y. What is the value of x - y?
NSAA-2018-M1-013: Q is 5 km away from P on a bearing of 065°. R is 5 km away from Q on a bearing of 155°. What is the bearing of P from R?
NSAA-2018-M1-014: With school lunch, students can select tomato sauce, or mayonnaise, or both, or neither. n students selected both. 3n + 1 students selected tomato sauce (in total). 3n - 1 students selected only mayonnaise. There were 7n + 5 students in the group. The probability of a student, chosen at random, selecting only mayonnaise is 1/3. By finding n, what is the probability of a student, chosen at random, selecting only tomato sauce?
NSAA-2018-M1-015: The line segment RT is a tangent at the point S to a circle with centre O [DIAGRAM: circle with tangent RT at S, points Q and P on the circumference, angle PST = 75°]. Q and P are points on the circumference of the circle such that QS = QP. Angle PST = 75°. What is the size of angle QSO?
NSAA-2018-M1-016: The vertical height h cm of an isosceles triangle is 3 cm longer than the base length of b cm. The sloping side is of length s cm. The area of the triangle is 14 cm2. There is one value of s which satisfies these conditions. Within which range does this value of s lie?
NSAA-2018-M1-017: The first five terms of a sequence in order are: 2, 17, 42, 77, 122. The nth term of this sequence is pn2 + q where p and q are integers. What is the value of (p + q)/(p - q)?
NSAA-2018-M1-018: A bag contains 6 red and 6 green sweets. The sweets are identical apart from their colour. A child takes a sweet at random from the bag. If the sweet is red, the child stops taking sweets. If the sweet is green, it is not replaced and the child takes another sweet. This continues until a red sweet is taken at which point the child stops taking sweets. What is the probability that the child takes more green sweets than red sweets?
NSAA-2018-PHY-001: An unstable nucleus X becomes a stable nucleus Y after a succession of decays, during which a total of 5 alpha particles and 2 beta (β-) particles are emitted. How many fewer protons does nucleus Y contain than nucleus X?
NSAA-2018-PHY-002: The diagram shows three resistors R1, R2 and R3 connected in series with a battery of constant voltage. R1 = 12Ω, R2 = 15Ω, R3 = 3.0Ω, and the current in the circuit is 0.20A. Resistor R3 is now removed and the circuit is reconnected. What is the new current in the circuit?
NSAA-2018-PHY-003: When travelling in a vacuum, visible light has a wavelength between $400\text{ nm}$ and $700\text{ nm}$. The speed of light in a vacuum is $3.0\times10^8\ \text{m s}^{-1}$. What can be concluded about ultraviolet radiation from this information?
NSAA-2018-PHY-004: [DIAGRAM: speed-time graph for a bus travelling in a straight line between two stops, speed axis 0-14 m/s, time axis 0-120s, showing acceleration, constant speed, and deceleration phases] What is the average speed of the bus during this time?
NSAA-2018-PHY-005: A filament lamp working at its operating voltage converts electrical energy at a rate of 100W. The lamp has an efficiency of 5.0%. How much energy is wasted by the lamp in 10 minutes?
NSAA-2018-PHY-006: A student is investigating heat flow along a solid uniform metal bar. The bar has length l, cross-sectional area A, and has its ends maintained at temperatures T1 and T2 (where T1 > T2). Which relationship represents the rate of heat flow P along the bar? (Assume that there is no heat transfer through the sides of the bar.)
NSAA-2018-PHY-007: The potential difference across the motor in an electric car is 400V and the current in the motor is 1250A. The car accelerates along a horizontal road from rest for 4.0 s. The efficiency of the overall system is 45%. What is the kinetic energy of the car at the end of the 4.0 s? (Ignore energy losses due to air resistance and due to friction between the tyres and the road.)
NSAA-2018-PHY-008: The momentum of a small object moving in a straight line is 24 kg ms-1 and its kinetic energy is 96 J. What is the mass of the object?
NSAA-2018-PHY-009: A radioactive isotope decays in a single step to a stable isotope. A radiation detector is placed very near to a sample of the radioactive isotope in a laboratory. [DIAGRAM: count rate vs time graph, count rate axis 0-150 counts/min, time axis 0-400 minutes, showing exponential decay curve levelling off]. What is the background count rate and what is the half-life of the isotope?
NSAA-2018-PHY-010: A rock falling vertically experiences an air resistance force of 12 N at an instant when its acceleration is 2.0 ms-2 downwards. What is the mass of the rock? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg-1)
NSAA-2018-PHY-011: A transverse wave with an amplitude of 4.0 cm and a frequency of 10 Hz travels along a rope at a speed of 2.4 ms-1. What is the total distance travelled by a particle in the rope in a time of 20 s?
NSAA-2018-PHY-012: A student places a measuring cylinder on a balance. She pours a volume V of water into the measuring cylinder, and finds that the mass of the measuring cylinder and water together is 290g. She then empties the measuring cylinder and dries it before putting it back on the balance. She now pours the same volume V of olive oil into the measuring cylinder, and finds that the mass of the measuring cylinder and olive oil together is 270g. What is the mass of the measuring cylinder? (densities: olive oil = 0.90 g cm-3; water = 1.0 g cm-3)
NSAA-2018-PHY-013: A skydiver of weight 1000N falls vertically. [DIAGRAM: distance-time graph for the skydiver, distance axis 0-500m, time axis 0-12s, curve approaching a straight line (terminal velocity) at large t]. The air resistance $F$ (in $\text{N}$) acting on the skydiver travelling at velocity $v$ (in $\text{m s}^{-1}$) is given by the equation $F = kv^2$ where $k$ (in $\text{N m}^{-2}\,\text{s}^2$) is a constant. What is the numerical value of $k$ for the skydiver?
NSAA-2018-PHY-014: A source generates water waves of fixed frequency that have a wavelength of 1.5 cm. As they cross a boundary into shallower water their frequency does not change, but their speed is reduced by 18 cms-1. The new wavelength is 1.2 cm. What is the speed of the waves in the shallower water?
NSAA-2018-PHY-015: A neutron is absorbed by a uranium-235 (U-235, Z=92) nuclide. The resulting nuclide undergoes fission to produce a bromine-88 (Br-88, Z=35) nuclide, a lanthanum-145 nuclide and some neutrons. The lanthanum-145 nuclide is radioactive and emits a beta (β-) particle. How many neutrons are emitted in the fission reaction and how many protons are there in the nuclide formed by the decay of lanthanum-145?
NSAA-2018-PHY-016: The diagram shows a circuit containing a battery and three identical resistors X, Y and Z [DIAGRAM: X in series with the battery; Y and Z in parallel with each other, this parallel combination in series with X]. The total power supplied by the battery is 18W. What is the power dissipated as heat in resistor X?
NSAA-2018-PHY-017: A filament lamp and a 0-10Ω variable resistor are connected in series with a power supply of constant voltage. [DIAGRAM: voltage-current (V-I) characteristic curve of the filament lamp, V axis 0-25V, I axis 0-4A, showing typical curved (non-ohmic) filament lamp behaviour]. When the resistance of the variable resistor is 4.0Ω, the current in the lamp is 2.0A. What is the power dissipated in the lamp when the resistance of the variable resistor is zero?
NSAA-2018-PHY-018: Three detectors X, Y and Z are separated by large distances. Each of the detectors records a seismic wave from the same earthquake whose epicentre (source) is very close to the surface of the Earth. The wave travels out from the epicentre at 4.0 kms-1. Detectors X and Y start to detect the wave at the same time, but detector Z starts to detect it one minute later. Which of the following statements must be correct? 1. The epicentre is at the midpoint of the line XY. 2. Z is equidistant from X and Y. 3. Z is no more than 240 km away from X and from Y.
NSAA-2018-CHEM-001: Which row in the following table gives the numbers of protons, neutrons and electrons in Cu-64 2+ (atomic number 29)?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-002: The following exothermic reaction reaches equilibrium at room temperature: C2H5OH(l) + CH3COOH(l) ⇌ CH3COOC2H5(l) + H2O(l). Which of the following changes, when applied independently, will alter the position of the equilibrium? 1. increasing the temperature by 25°C. 2. adding 20cm3 of water to the equilibrium mixture. 3. adding a catalyst. 4. adding an extra 0.5 mol of ethanol (C2H5OH).
NSAA-2018-CHEM-003: What is the overall process that takes place at the cathode (negative electrode) in the electrolysis of dilute aqueous sodium sulfate?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-004: A mass spectrum of a sample of element X with atomic number 5 is shown [DIAGRAM: mass spectrum with peaks at masses 10 and 11 showing relative abundances]. Using the data, which row in the following table best describes the position of X in the Periodic Table and the relative atomic mass of this sample of X?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-005: The gases nitrogen, oxygen and argon can be separated from liquefied air by fractional distillation. Given the data: nitrogen (mp -210°C, bp -196°C), oxygen (mp -218°C, bp -183°C), argon (mp -189°C, bp -186°C). In which order would the gases be collected?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-006: Concentrated aqueous sodium chloride was electrolysed. After a few minutes, the remaining electrolyte solution was tested with a pH probe at 25°C. The gases produced at the electrodes were collected and tested with a colourless aqueous solution of sodium bromide. Which row in the following table best describes the observations in these tests?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-007: The following equations show the main reactions in a blast furnace: C + O2 → CO2; CO2 + C → 2CO; Fe2O3 + 3CO → 2Fe + 3CO2; CaCO3 → CaO + CO2; CaO + SiO2 → CaSiO3. Which row correctly identifies whether the underlined substance (CO2 in the second equation, CaCO3, and CaO) is oxidised, reduced, or neither?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-008: X, Y and Z have the same electron configuration. X is an atom, Y is a monatomic anion and Z is a monatomic cation. Which of the following statements is always correct?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-009: In the Contact process, sulfur dioxide reacts with oxygen to make sulfur trioxide in a reversible reaction: SO2(g) + 1/2 O2(g) ⇌ SO3(g). When 5.00 mol of SO2 and 11.0 mol of O2 are allowed to reach equilibrium at 450°C, 80.0% of the SO2 is converted to SO3. What is the volume of the resulting mixture? (Assume that at this temperature the volume of one mole of gas is 60.0 dm3.)
NSAA-2018-CHEM-010: The non-metallic element phosphorus forms two stable chlorides: PCl3 (boiling point 76°C) and PCl5 (boiling point 161°C). Which of the following statements explain(s) the difference in boiling points? 1. There are more covalent bonds in PCl5 so more energy is required to break them. 2. The forces between the molecules in liquid PCl5 are stronger. 3. The covalent bonds in PCl3 are weaker so less energy is required to break them.
NSAA-2018-CHEM-011: A student calculated the mass of anhydrous copper(II) sulfate (CuSO4) required to make 250cm3 of an aqueous solution of concentration 0.200 mol dm-3. However, the student mistakenly made the solution using the same mass of hydrated copper(II) sulfate (CuSO4·5H2O) instead. What is the concentration, in mol dm-3, of the solution made with the hydrated copper(II) sulfate? (Ar: Cu=64; S=32; O=16; H=1.0)
NSAA-2018-CHEM-012: Bromine is an element in Group 17 of the Periodic Table. Which of the following statements is/are correct about the element bromine? 1. Bromine will oxidise chloride ions in aqueous solution to form chlorine. 2. Bromine has a lower boiling point than chlorine. 3. Bromine reacts with calcium (Group 2) to form a compound containing 80% bromine by mass. (Ar: Cl=35.5; Ca=40; Br=80)
NSAA-2018-CHEM-013: Paper chromatography was used to separate three mixtures of amino acids, labelled P, Q and R [DIAGRAM: chromatogram showing spots for mixtures P, Q, R]. Rf values: asparagine 0.50, glutamic acid 0.30, glycine 0.26, leucine 0.71, valine 0.61. Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Mixture P contains valine and glycine. 2. Leucine is found in all three mixtures. 3. Glutamic acid is the least mobile amino acid with this solvent. 4. Mixtures P and Q both contain asparagine.
NSAA-2018-CHEM-014: Dilute hydrochloric acid and magnesium were mixed and the total volume of gas released was measured over time [DIAGRAM: volume of gas (cm3) vs time (s) graph, showing volume rising from 0 to about 40cm3 over 10s, with volume at t=2s reading approximately 16cm3]. What is the average rate of reaction, in g s-1, with respect to the magnesium over the first two seconds? (Ar: Mg=24. Assume 1 mole of gas occupies 24 dm3.)
NSAA-2018-CHEM-015: Copper can react with concentrated nitric acid to form the gas nitrogen monoxide. 3Cu + aHNO3 → bCu(NO3)2 + cH2O + dNO. What is the value of a when the equation is balanced?
NSAA-2018-CHEM-016: A small amount of a solid mixture, containing calcium carbonate and an inert substance, was added to 50.00cm3 dilute hydrochloric acid of concentration 0.1000 mol dm-3. CaCO3 + 2HCl → CaCl2 + CO2 + H2O. After all of the calcium carbonate had reacted, the solution was heated to drive off the carbon dioxide. The resulting solution was neutralised by 12.50cm3 of 0.1000 mol dm-3 sodium hydroxide solution. What was the mass of calcium carbonate in the mixture? (Mr: CaCO3=100.0)
NSAA-2018-CHEM-017: 2.80g of lithium metal is placed in a closed system with 1.20dm3 of pure oxygen gas (volume measured at room temperature and pressure). If a complete reaction occurs between the lithium and the oxygen, what is the maximum mass of lithium oxide that can be formed? (Ar: Li=7; O=16. Assume 1 mole of gas occupies 24.0dm3 at RTP.)
NSAA-2018-CHEM-018: The following reaction between nitrogen oxide and oxygen releases 116kJ of energy as heat for each mole of oxygen that reacts: 2NO(g) + O2(g) ⇌ 2NO2(g). An excess of NO and y moles of oxygen are mixed in a sealed container. The reaction reaches equilibrium in one hour. At equilibrium, there are z moles of NO2. Assume that the pressure is constant throughout the experiment. How much heat will be released over this hour?
NSAA-2018-BIO-001: The graph shows how four variables (algae, fish, oxygen, bacteria) change with the distance down a river, after a source of pollution flowed in [DIAGRAM: line graph showing oxygen dropping sharply then recovering, bacteria peaking near the pollution source, algae and fish patterns downstream, with three marked points 1, 2, 3 along the distance axis]. Which indicator species could be expected to be found in large numbers at points 1, 2 and 3?
NSAA-2018-BIO-002: Sickle cell anaemia is a recessive genetic condition that results in abnormally-shaped red blood cells due to the production of a faulty type of haemoglobin. Children born with sickle cell anaemia rarely live to adulthood without significant medical intervention. Carriers, who only have one copy of the sickle cell allele, have greater resistance to the disease malaria than people with two copies of the allele for normal functional haemoglobin. Using this information, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. People with sickle cell anaemia would have reduced anaerobic respiration in their muscle cells. 2. In areas with malaria the percentage of people surviving with sickle cell anaemia increases. 3. In parts of Africa where malaria is more common you would expect to find more people with a sickle cell allele.
NSAA-2018-BIO-003: A student studied this photograph of part of an organ, in which two cells are labelled X and Y [DIAGRAM: micrograph of an organ showing two labelled cells X and Y]. The student drew the following conclusions: 1. Both cells X and Y are found in the same tissue. 2. Both cells X and Y were produced by mitosis. 3. Both cells X and Y have a cell wall. Which of these conclusions is/are correct?
NSAA-2018-BIO-004: A student investigated the tadpole population in a large pond. A net with a rectangular opening measuring 0.1m x 0.2m was swept through the water for a fixed distance of 1m. This was repeated 10 times. All the sweeps were made at the edge of the pond as the student had no waders or boat. The number of tadpoles in each sweep: 20, 12, 32, 0, 4, 8, 4, 8, 12, 20. The student made the following statements: 1. Each sweep sampled 0.02 m3 of water. 2. The frequency of occurrence of the tadpoles was 90%. 3. An accurate estimate of the population size of tadpoles in the pond could be calculated using this data if the total volume of water was known. Which of the statements about the investigation is/are correct?
NSAA-2018-BIO-005: A breeding experiment was carried out using rats. A pair of rats has eight offspring per litter. The offspring breed freely amongst each other within the same generation. Each female is only allowed to have one litter of eight. The expected ratio of male to female offspring in this breeding population is the same in rats as in humans (1:1), and is seen in every generation. In the 4th generation of offspring, how many individuals would be expected to have the XY genotype (i.e. be male)?
NSAA-2018-BIO-006: The diagram shows four steps in the process of human sperm production: early spermatogonium →(mitosis)→ intermediate spermatogonium →(mitosis)→ late spermatogonium →(mitosis)→ primary spermatocytes →(meiosis)→ sperm. For each step only one complete division takes place. Assuming no mutations and that all of the cells survive, what will be the maximum number of haploid cells originating from a single early spermatogonium?
NSAA-2018-BIO-007: Which of the following conditions is/are required by the cells near the tip of a plant shoot in order for the tip to grow towards light from one direction? 1. sufficient glucose. 2. uneven distribution of plant hormone. 3. sufficient oxygen.
NSAA-2018-BIO-008: A cell from the epithelium of an animal was removed. The cytoplasm of this cell can be considered as a 2% sugar solution. The living cell was placed in a 4% sugar solution. Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. At equilibrium, the sugar concentration in the cell was 6%. 2. Water continued to move across the cell membrane after equilibrium was reached. 3. Osmosis was most rapid when the cell was first placed in the solution.
NSAA-2018-BIO-009: A piece of DNA is made up of two complementary strands, each 25 bases long (50 bases total). 14% of the total bases are adenine. Which two statements are correct? 1. Adenine and cytosine together make up 25 bases. 2. Adenine and guanine together make up 50% of the bases. 3. There are 14 thymine bases present. 4. 36 of the bases are guanine.
NSAA-2018-BIO-010: In a laboratory, the activity of two lipase enzymes on the same type of lipid was studied. One lipase enzyme was produced from a mutation in the gene that coded for the original enzyme. The mutation occurred in the sequence for three adjacent amino acids called serine, aspartic acid and histidine. The graph shows the results of this study, with points P (early, lipase) and Q (later, mutated lipase) marked [DIAGRAM: total mass of product vs time graph comparing lipase and mutated lipase reaction curves]. Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. The serine, aspartic acid and histidine amino acids could be in the active site of the enzyme. 2. All mutations affecting the region coding for serine, aspartic acid and histidine amino acids will be expected to have the same effect. 3. At point Q on the graph, the pH of the reaction mixture will be higher than at P.
NSAA-2018-BIO-011: The family tree shows a family affected by a dominant genetic condition. All people who carry the mutation show symptoms of the condition [DIAGRAM: pedigree/family tree showing individuals P, Q, and female R, with symbols for males/females with/without the condition]. Which of the following statements could explain the presence of the dominant condition in female R? 1. The mutation occurs in P. 2. The mutation occurs in Q's father. 3. The mutation occurs in R.
NSAA-2018-BIO-012: Dolly the sheep was born in 1996. She was unusual because she had no biological father. The diagram shows how she was produced: a nucleus from a cultured mammary gland cell was fused with an enucleated egg cell using an electric shock, forming an early embryo which was implanted into a surrogate mother and developed into the lamb Dolly [DIAGRAM: somatic cell nuclear transfer process]. Which of the following processes had to occur to produce Dolly? 1. genetic engineering. 2. mitosis. 3. meiosis. 4. differentiation.
NSAA-2018-BIO-013: A student investigated the rate of oxygen bubble release from a pondweed plant at different temperatures. The rates are shown below, with two sections of the graph marked X and Y [DIAGRAM: rate of oxygen bubble release vs temperature graph, X = rising section at lower temperatures, Y = declining section at higher temperatures]. In each investigation all other factors were kept constant. Which of the following statements about sections X and Y is/are correct? 1. In section X, the kinetic energy of the reaction molecules is increasing with increasing temperature. 2. In section Y, temperature is the factor which limits that rate of oxygen production. 3. In section Y, the plant's enzymes may have denatured. 4. Section Y represents the plant's maximum possible rate of oxygen production under any conditions.
NSAA-2018-BIO-014: The diagram below shows a circular piece of bacterial DNA called a plasmid that has been made recombinant by the insertion of a plant gene for herbicide resistance, inserted into the middle of the gene coding for resistance to the antibiotic tetracycline (splitting it into a first and second part), while a separate gene codes for resistance to the antibiotic penicillin [DIAGRAM: plasmid map showing herbicide resistance gene, penicillin resistance gene, and disrupted tetracycline resistance gene]. Bacteria containing only the recombinant plasmid had to be identified from bacteria that contained only the original plasmid (with intact penicillin and tetracycline resistance genes). These bacteria were grown on agar containing penicillin, and separately on agar containing tetracycline. Which row correctly shows the growth of these bacteria on the two sets of agar?
NSAA-2018-BIO-015: A student analysed a gene sequence identified in four different organisms (human, oak tree, mushroom, E. coli bacterium), coding for a functional protein. A section of the DNA is shown: human ACGCCTCGTCACGCTAAA; oak tree ACGGAATATGTAGCTAAA; mushroom ACGGAACTCTTAGCTAAA; E.coli ACGTACGATGGGGCTAAA. The rest of the DNA (not shown) is identical in all four organisms. The student made the following conclusions: 1. This gene does not code for chlorophyll. 2. This gene may be found in the nucleus or cytoplasm. 3. The protein that this gene codes for is likely to be more similar in plants and fungi than in the other organisms. Which of these conclusions is/are correct?
NSAA-2018-BIO-016: The graphs represent the changes in the mass of a healthy human body cell and in the mass of the DNA of that cell over time, from 0 to 60 hours [DIAGRAM: two line graphs, mass of cell body and mass of DNA in a cell, both vs time in hours, showing DNA mass doubling then cell mass halving at division points]. Using the graph, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Mitosis takes place at 12 and 36 hours. 2. The graph shows two cell divisions. 3. The next cell division should take place at 72 hours.
NSAA-2018-BIO-017: Bt pesticide is used by farmers to kill insect pests. However, widespread use has resulted in the evolution of resistance to this pesticide. A recessive allele causes resistance. Scientists have suggested that in areas where the Bt pesticide is used, a small number of fields are left untreated (refugia). This method has been shown to slow down evolution of resistance to the pesticide. Which of the following statements explain why refugia could slow down the evolution of resistance to Bt pesticide? 1. When resistant insects breed with pesticide-sensitive insects that do not have the allele for resistance, the offspring produced will be sensitive to the pesticide. 2. When fewer insects are exposed to pesticide, fewer mutations occur that produce alleles for resistance. 3. The refugia help to maintain genetic variation in the population of insect pests.
NSAA-2018-BIO-018: A diploid plant cell divides by mitosis. After mitosis of this cell, a mutation occurs that changes the genotype of one of the daughter cells. This mutant daughter cell produces a non-functional enzyme instead of the functional enzyme produced by the other daughter cell. This mutation has no effect on the phenotype of the plant or the number or length of chromosomes in the plant cell. Which of the following statements describe the daughter cells after the mutation has occurred? 1. The chromosomes in the nucleus of each daughter cell will contain the same genes. 2. Both daughter cells will contain the same alleles for every gene. 3. The sequence of bases along each allele will be the same in each daughter cell.
NSAA-2018-M1-019: Curve $C$ has equation $y = 9 - x^2$. Line $L$ has equation $y = 5$. What is the area enclosed between $C$ and $L$?
NSAA-2018-PHY-019: An aircraft moves from rest with uniform acceleration along a horizontal runway. After travelling 1600m it reaches a speed of 80 ms-1. What is the acceleration of the aircraft?
NSAA-2018-M2-001: How many solutions of the equation $2\sin^3\theta = \sin\theta$ lie in the interval $-\tfrac{\pi}{2} \le \theta \le \pi$?
NSAA-2018-PHY-020: The diagram represents a mass that is moving in a straight line at constant speed up a slope of constant gradient [DIAGRAM: mass on an inclined slope moving at constant speed]. Which statement about the forces acting on the mass must be correct?
NSAA-2018-M2-002: The line $y = x + k$, where $k$ is a constant, is a tangent to the curve $y = 3x^2 - 2x + 1$. What is the value of $k$?
NSAA-2018-PHY-021: The diagram shows four objects W, X, Y and Z, of masses 3.0kg, 4.0kg, 6.0kg and 2.0kg respectively, connected in a line by light, inextensible rods in the order W-X-Y-Z [DIAGRAM: four blocks connected by rods on a smooth horizontal surface, with a 30N force applied at X pulling the whole system]. The objects are pulled along a smooth, horizontal surface by a constant force of 30N. What is the tension in the rod connecting X and Y?
NSAA-2018-M2-003: In a particular arithmetic progression: the 13th term is six times the 1st term; the 11th term is 1 less than twice the 5th term. What is the 3rd term of the progression?
NSAA-2018-PHY-022: An object of mass 40kg is placed on a uniform, horizontal plank of mass 10kg between two supports X and Y. The object is 1.0m from X; the centre of the plank is a further 1.0m from the object; and support Y is a further 2.0m beyond the plank's centre [DIAGRAM: plank on two supports X and Y with object placed off-centre]. What is the contact force at X? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg-1)
NSAA-2018-PHY-023: An object X of mass 2.0kg is initially moving at a speed of 4.5 ms-1 on a smooth, horizontal surface. A 5.0N force is applied to X in the direction of its motion for 3.0 seconds. A short time later it collides head on with, and sticks to, a stationary object Y of mass 3.0kg. What is the speed of X and Y as they move off together after the collision?
NSAA-2018-M2-005: Circle $C$ has equation $(x + 3)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = 5$. The length of the tangent from the circle $C$ to the point $P$ is $5\sqrt{3}$. What is the shortest distance from $P$ to $C$?
NSAA-2018-PHY-024: Two solid spheres X and Y have masses m and 2m respectively. They travel in opposite directions towards each other along the same line with speeds v and 2v respectively and collide head on. The graph shows the variation of velocity with time for sphere X before, during, and after the collision: X starts at +v, and after the collision moves at -v [DIAGRAM: velocity-time graph for sphere X]. Which sketch shows the variation of velocity with time for sphere Y?
NSAA-2018-M2-006: What is the coefficient of $x^3$ in the expansion of $(1 - 2x)^5 (1 + 2x)^5$?
NSAA-2018-PHY-025: A metal ball suspended from a steel cable is held at rest by a horizontal force P. The cable makes an angle of 30° to the vertical as shown in the diagram [DIAGRAM: ball on cable at 30° to vertical, held by horizontal force P, cable tension T]. What is the magnitude of P?
NSAA-2018-M2-007: Given that $\displaystyle\int_0^{2} x^{m}\,dx = \frac{16\sqrt{2}}{7}$ and $\displaystyle\int_0^{2} x^{m+1}\,dx = \frac{32\sqrt{2}}{9}$, what is the value of $m$?
NSAA-2018-PHY-026: A pendulum bob of mass 10g is suspended by a light, inextensible string of length 50cm. The bob is released from rest at position X [DIAGRAM: pendulum with string length 50cm, showing bob released from X, swinging down to a lower point Y, with a horizontal offset of 30cm marked]. What is the speed of the bob as it passes through position Y? (gravitational field strength g = 10 N kg-1; assume resistive forces are negligible)
NSAA-2018-M1-020: The dimensions of a solid cuboid, in cm, are x, 2x and y. The volume of the cuboid is 576cm3. At this volume, the surface area of the cuboid has its maximum value. What is the area, in cm2, of the face that has the largest area?
NSAA-2018-PHY-027: An object is thrown vertically upwards from ground level with an initial velocity of 40 m s-1. 2.0 seconds later another object is released from a height above the ground and falls vertically from rest. Both of the objects hit the ground at the same time. From what height above the ground was the second object released? (gravitational field strength g = 10 N kg-1; air resistance can be ignored)
